mnemonics: Think of the Three Schema Architecture as a three-layer building: External (user view), Conceptual (architect's blueprint), and Internal (builder's foundation).
keyPoints: A Database Schema is the overall design/logical structure of the database.
A Database Instance is the actual data stored at a specific moment in time.
Logical Data Independence protects application programs from changes in the conceptual schema.
Physical Data Independence protects the conceptual schema from changes in the internal schema/physical storage.
commonMistakes: Confusing Schema (the design) with Instance (the actual data).
Mixing up Logical Independence (changes in tables/views) with Physical Independence (changes in files/indexes).
